title: gastrolobium triangulare
intro: Gastrolobium triangulare, also known as the Stinking Git, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its clusters of small, white or pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gastrolobium triangulare are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat various infections. Gastrolobium triangulare can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity.
